hard.next_step: Got in touch with Blake Miller again, he is currently in internal discussions with his team, setup a deeper discovery for first week of August Need to setup a deeper discovery call in a week, the prospect will get back to us about their initiative
hard.ais_why:
• Carmeuse is early: next step is a 'Deeper discovery call setup for first week of August,' with no discovery notes yet captured.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped, indicating AI-enabled modules are in the frame, but no agentic discussion or sentiment is recorded.
• Evidence is limited to product tags and a scheduling step, so AI appetite cannot be placed above exploratory.

north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded early Qualified S2P suite deal that has gone quiet since the 26 May intro meeting; the early-August deeper discovery is the make-or-break to build MEDDPICC depth or qualify out.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-05-26, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-29, so 7 days in Qualified as of 2026-07-06. Close date 2027-05-01 is roughly 300 days out, so there is no date pressure. The problem is engagement, not the calendar: the only genuine two-way touch was the 26 May intro meeting, a 2026-06-29 health task records 'no inbound since last 14 days,' and the deeper discovery is still unbooked. No competitor, economic buyer, champion, or quantified pain is on record, so momentum, not timing, is the risk.
evidence: Only one genuine two-way buyer touch on record: the 26 May 2026 intro meeting, followed by the Clari intro email that thanks Blake Miller 'for taking the time to meet earlier today and for sharing where Carmeuse is on your SAP and AI journey' (SF Task, Clari email 2026-05-26).

Next step is rep-initiated and pending, not completed engagement: 'he is currently in internal discussions with his team, setup a deeper discovery for first week of August ... the prospect will get back to us about their initiative' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29).
A 2026-06-29 Opportunity Health task flags 'Prospect's gone cold? No inbound since last 14 days,' confirming buyer engagement has thinned.
Zero Avoma calls discovered for the opp or the Carmeuse account, so there are no buyer voices, no MEDDPICC evidence, and no competitive intel from transcripts.
Single-threaded: Blake Miller is the only Carmeuse person on any activity, and there are no OpportunityContactRole entries, so no economic buyer, decision maker, or champion is mapped.

Broad footprint for the price: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing, Merlin, and Merlin Intake scoped at $110K, implying a phased roll-out or a small user base rather than a full big-bang suite spend.
Pipeline forecast is defensible: the deal is roughly 300 days out, 7 days into Qualified, with discovery not yet run, so it is premature to promote it to Best Case.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case is on record. The only substantive signal is the 26 May 2026 Clari intro email, which thanks Blake Miller for 'sharing where Carmeuse is on your SAP and AI journey' and gives a Zycus overview of an organically built source-to-pay platform, so we know Carmeuse is pursuing an SAP-linked digital and AI transformation but we have not heard the operational or financial pain, the cost of inaction, or any success metric in the buyer's words. No Avoma calls exist to extract savings targets, cycle-time, or headcount impact. Quantifying the S2P business case (spend under management, contract cycle time, invoice error rate, savings goal) is the primary objective of the early-August deeper discovery.
sources: SF Task, Clari intro email 2026-05-26 ('sharing where Carmeuse is on your SAP and AI journey')
No Avoma calls discovered (opp + account pull)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. There are no OpportunityContactRole records, and no MEDDPICC or budget field names a budget owner. Blake Miller is 'in internal discussions with his team' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), which implies budget authority sits with someone above or beside him whom we have not met. The evidence shows nothing yet about who signs at Carmeuse, so identifying the budget owner and securing an introduction is a ranked move for the August discovery; naming an economic buyer now would be a fabrication.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29 ('internal discussions with his team')

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are not documented. No RFP, RFI, ShoeFit, or workshop has run, and no Avoma call or requirement field carries a stated criterion. What we can infer from the 26 May intro email is that Carmeuse frames this against their 'SAP and AI journey,' so alignment with their existing SAP environment and the maturity of the AI/Merlin capability are likely to be criteria, but neither is confirmed by the buyer. Surfacing the explicit decision criteria (must-have modules, integration requirements, AI expectations) is a core discovery objective.
sources: No RFP/RFI or Avoma evidence on record

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is not yet visible. The only clue is that Blake Miller is running 'internal discussions with his team' about 'their initiative' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), which tells us Carmeuse is still shaping the initiative internally before opening a formal evaluation. There is no evidence of an evaluation timeline, an approval chain, or who joins at which stage because no multi-person call has occurred. Mapping the process (who decides, what stages, what timeline) is a discovery deliverable.
sources: SF StageName history: Initial Interest to Qualified 2026-06-29

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view, which is expected this early. This is a New Business deal (Type set to the relevant stakeholder on 2026-06-29), so a full-suite S2P win would eventually need an MSA plus Order Form and a separately signed SOW, but nothing about legal, procurement channel, InfoSec, or a contract-expiration forcing function is known. Visibility here will only come after discovery and a shortlist decision; it is not a current risk at Qualified stage.
sources: SF Field History: Type to New Business 2026-06-29

identify_pain.narrative: The specific pain is not yet articulated by the buyer. The 26 May intro email positions Zycus against Carmeuse's 'SAP and AI journey' and their interest in an organically built source-to-pay platform, which points to a modernization or consolidation driver, but the concrete pain (manual contracting, fragmented suppliers, poor spend visibility, invoice reconciliation) has not been stated on any call or in any field. The broad module scope (iContract through eInvoicing plus Merlin) implies pain across the S2P chain, but that is inference, not confirmed pain. The pain owner is presumed to be Blake Miller's team; confirming and quantifying the pain is the make-or-break discovery objective.

champion.narrative: No proven champion. Blake Miller is our only relationship and is doing internal work on our behalf ('in internal discussions with his team,' SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), which is an encouraging early signal, but his title, seniority, and access to the budget owner are unknown, and no Avoma call exists to observe him advocating, sharing internal criteria, or introducing other stakeholders. He is a single, unverified point of contact, not yet a champion. The August discovery must test his access and influence and, if he is credible, arm him with the Merlin AI and unified-S2P story to sell internally; if he is weak, multi-threading is urgent.
sources: SF Task, Clari intro email 2026-05-26 (addressed to Blake)

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c and the free-text competition fields are empty, no Avoma call exists, and no task or event references a rival. The single adjacent signal is that Carmeuse is on an 'SAP and AI journey' (Clari intro email 2026-05-26), which raises but does not confirm the question of whether they will default to the relevant stakeholder as the incumbent-ecosystem S2P path. It is unknown whether this is a sole-source look at Zycus or a quiet multi-vendor evaluation. Establishing the competitive set, and specifically whether SAP Ariba is the internal default, is a top discovery objective; the likely Zycus wedge is Merlin's agentic negotiation and intake automation plus a unified organically-built platform, but it cannot be activated until we know who we are compared against.
sources: SF Task, Clari intro email 2026-05-26 ('SAP and AI journey')

deal_movement.summary: The deal was created 2026-05-26 with owner reassigned to Edward Dlugosz, held an intro meeting with Blake Miller the same day, then advanced Initial Interest to Qualified and was tagged New Business on 2026-06-29. Since the 26 May intro meeting there has been no buyer inbound (health task 2026-06-29 flags 14+ days cold), and the deeper discovery for early August is still to be booked.

items.change: Intro meeting held with Blake Miller; Zycus overview of organically built source-to-pay shared, covering Carmeuse's SAP and AI journey
Opportunity created and owner reassigned to Edward Dlugosz
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified
Type set to New Business; Division moved to New Markets
Health task flags prospect cold, no inbound in 14+ days

critical_signals.text: The 26 May intro meeting covered Carmeuse's SAP and AI journey, but no buyer inbound has followed and the deeper discovery is still unbooked.
Blake Miller is the only Carmeuse person we have engaged and we have never met a second stakeholder, leaving the deal fully single-threaded.
No rival is named yet; the open question is whether Carmeuse defaults to SAP Ariba given their stated SAP journey, which the August discovery must surface.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. No competitor appears in Competitors__c, the free-text competition fields, any task, or any Avoma call (none exist). The only adjacent signal is that Carmeuse is on an 'SAP and AI journey' (Clari intro email 2026-05-26), which flags SAP Ariba as a plausible incumbent-ecosystem default but does not confirm it as an active rival. Until the early-August discovery, we do not know whether this is a sole-source look at Zycus or a quiet multi-vendor evaluation. The likely Zycus wedge for a $110K S2P suite with Merlin is agentic negotiation and intake automation plus a single organically-built platform versus stitched modules, but it cannot be activated until the competitive set is known.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Too early to assess fit. No requirements, use cases, or evaluation criteria have been captured because discovery has not run; the only anchor is Carmeuse's stated 'SAP and AI journey' from the 26 May intro email, implying expectations around SAP-environment fit and AI capability that remain unconfirmed by the buyer.

items.deliverable: Blake Miller to revert on Carmeuse's initiative and confirm the early-August deeper discovery date
items.grounding_quote: he is currently in internal discussions with his team ... the prospect will get back to us about their initiative

items.quote: sharing where Carmeuse is on your SAP and AI journey

items.source: SF Competitors__c empty; no Avoma calls

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified, single-threaded, and cooling: the win-strategy priorities are to lock the discovery, break single-threading, and pre-empt an SAP-default competitive posture.
flags: Book and hold the early-August deeper discovery with a written agenda tied to Carmeuse's SAP and AI journey, or the deal stalls in Qualified.
Multi-thread beyond Blake Miller into a procurement and an IT stakeholder before discovery to de-risk a single-threaded deal.
Lead with the Merlin agentic S2P wedge to differentiate early against a likely SAP Ariba default and test the competitive set.

items.name: Blake Miller
items.sentiment: Engaged but quiet (internal discussions underway per Next_Step__c 2026-06-29; no inbound in 14+ days)
items.risk: Only stakeholder on record and title/authority unknown; if Blake deprioritizes, loses budget, or leaves, the deal collapses. Single-threaded and unverified access to power.
items.source: SF Task, Clari intro email 2026-05-26; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29

champion_strength.summary: No proven champion. Blake Miller is our sole contact and is doing internal work on our behalf, which is a positive early sign, but his title, seniority, and access to the budget owner are unknown and no call exists to observe his advocacy. He is an unverified single point of contact, not yet a champion; the August discovery must test his influence and, if credible, arm him with the Merlin AI and unified-S2P story, or multi-thread if he is weak.
champion_strength.source: SF Task, Clari intro email 2026-05-26; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29; no Avoma calls

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped, but no agentic or AI value discussion is on record, so the AI story is present in the product tags yet under-positioned with the buyer. Carmeuse's own 'SAP and AI journey' framing is an opening to land the Merlin agentic narrative at discovery.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 4.0 / Moderate: AI-enabled modules (Merlin, Merlin Intake) are scoped and Carmeuse describes an active 'SAP and AI journey,' but no agentic discussion or sentiment is recorded, so appetite is exploratory rather than proven. No calls exist to confirm or upgrade the field read.

items.detail: Fully single-threaded on Blake Miller, whose authority is unknown; no second stakeholder engaged.

Buyer engagement thinning: health task flags no inbound in 14+ days and the deeper discovery is still unbooked, a no-decision / stalled-initiative risk.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: one intro meeting on 26 May, then rep-side chasing with no buyer inbound in 14+ days as of 2026-06-29 and the discovery still to be booked.
items: No buyer inbound in 14+ days (SF health task 2026-06-29)
Deeper discovery for early August not yet confirmed (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29)

customer_preference.evidence: No stated lean either way; the only interaction is a 26 May intro meeting with a generic Zycus S2P overview and no buyer preference expressed.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or automation impact discussed; only a qualitative 'SAP and AI journey' framing from the 26 May intro email.

scope_change.from: Full S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eInvoicing) plus Merlin and Merlin Intake
scope_change.to: Full S2P suite plus Merlin and Merlin Intake
scope_change.detail: Scope is unchanged since creation; the full suite has been in scope throughout with no modules dropped.

expansion_context.note: New Business (Type set to New Business 2026-06-29); no prior Closed-Won deal on Carmeuse, so executive and stakeholder access must be earned, not inherited.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early, single-threaded $110K S2P suite deal that has gone quiet since the 26 May intro meeting; the make-or-break is whether Blake Miller's early-August discovery actually happens and opens a second thread into a budget owner.

win_position.text: This is a thin early Qualified deal: one intro meeting on 26 May, no economic buyer or champion mapped, no quantified pain, and no competitor known, so there is little to stand on until discovery runs.
Fully single-threaded on Blake Miller, whose title and authority are unknown, so if he deprioritizes or the internal 'initiative' stalls the deal collapses with no backup relationship.
Engagement is already cooling, with a 2026-06-29 health task flagging no buyer inbound in 14+ days and the deeper discovery still unbooked.

deal_momentum.text: Blake Miller reported he is in internal discussions with his team and agreed to a deeper discovery for the first week of August (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29), keeping the deal alive.
No buyer-pulled next meeting or customer-named date beyond the rep-proposed August discovery, and no senior stakeholder has joined, so momentum is rep-driven not buyer-driven.

customer_commitment.text: The only buyer commitment is a soft promise to 'get back to us about their initiative' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-29); no requirement, timeline, or budget has been shared.
No Avoma calls, no RFP, and no requirement fields populated, so there is no evidence the buyer has committed effort beyond a single intro meeting.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is inertia: Carmeuse keeps shaping its S2P initiative internally and the discovery slips, a no-decision risk rather than a loss to a named rival.
Latent competitive risk that Carmeuse defaults to SAP Ariba given their stated SAP journey (Clari email 2026-05-26); unconfirmed but must be tested at discovery.

champion_strength.evidence: Only Blake Miller engaged, title and authority unknown, no advocacy observed on any call.

customer_commitment.evidence: One intro meeting and a soft revert-later promise; no requirements, budget, or buyer-named dates.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer inbound in 14+ days; discovery unbooked and rep-driven.

items.expected_effect: Differentiates early against a likely SAP Ariba default and gives Blake internal ammunition to sustain the initiative.

commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: momentum 44 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 44 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 44 (slipping backward): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 44, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
